etymology.description: semantic 門(門) (mén, "door") + phonetic 柬(柬) (jiǎn, "card") -> block
etymology.interpretation: The character 闌(lán, "block") combines the semantic component 門(門) (mén, "door") with the phonetic component 柬(柬) (jiǎn, "card"). The semantic element suggests a gateway or passage that can be closed or blocked, while the phonetic element contributes its sound. This reflects the idea of a barrier or closure, hence the meaning "block."

examples.chinese: 河水拦住了我们的去路(河水攔住了我們的去路)。
examples.pinyin: Héshuǐ lán zhùle wǒmen de qùlù.

examples.chinese: 警察在道路上设立了拦截点(警察在道路上設立了攔截點)。
examples.pinyin: Jǐngchá zài dàolù shàng shèlìle lánjié diǎn.
examples.english: The police set up a blockade on the road.

examples.chinese: 他的计划被家人拦下来了(他的計劃被家人攔下來了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de jìhuà bèi jiārén lán xiàláile.
examples.english: His plan was blocked by his family.
